steps.text: Welcome to the E-6 Bar Tools demo. Today we're testing accordo_layout_panel — a single tool that controls all three VS Code collapsible areas: sidebar, bottom panel, and the right auxiliary bar.
Tool: accordo_layout_panel({ area, view?, action })
Before we start — the tool uses a state tracker. All areas start as 'unknown' because VS Code doesn't tell us if a panel is open or closed. The first action we take synchronises the state.
Journey 1: Agent closes the sidebar to give the user more code space.
The sidebar closes. If it was already closed, this is a no-op — the tool is idempotent. The response tells us the action taken and the previous state.
Journey 2: Agent opens the sidebar and switches to the Git Source Control view.
Sidebar opens and the Git view becomes active. The view parameter works for all five sidebar views: explorer, search, git, debug, and extensions.
Journey 3: Agent opens the bottom panel to show the terminal.

The bottom panel opens and the Terminal tab is active. Notice we use a focus-first pattern for panel views: we open the panel first, then switch to the terminal. This avoids accidentally hiding the panel.
Journey 4: Same panel, but the agent wants to see build output.
The panel was already open — so we just switched from the Terminal view to the Output view. No hiding, no drama.
Journey 5: Agent opens the right auxiliary bar.
The right sidebar opens. The right bar doesn't have predefined views — users drag whatever they want there. That's why the view parameter is not supported for rightBar.
Error case 1: Agent tries to close a specific view — which is not allowed.
The tool returns a clear error: you cannot close a view, only the area. To close the sidebar, omit the view parameter.
Error case 2: Agent tries to specify a view for the rightBar.
Another clear error: rightBar does not support the view parameter. Use area-level open or close only.
Closing everything down — sidebar, panel, rightBar — to restore editor space.
All three areas are now closed. Clean code space. The state tracker remembers these states — so the next open call will know the areas are closed and won't do a no-op.
That was the E-6 Bar Tools demo. Summary: one tool, three areas, explicit open and close, state tracker for idempotency, view-level control for sidebar and panel, clear error messages.
